title: Quadlet fails to add host.containers.internal on boot
body:
### Issue Description

Quadlet fails to add host.containers.internal or host.docker.internal to /etc/hosts in the container on boot.
This happens both with Network set to a bridge network or a slirp4netns.
If the service is restarted after boot, the hosts are added correctly.
This affects all quadlets I have, no just the example.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Quadlet started at boot:
```

[Unit]
Description=protonmail-bridge container service
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

[Container]
Image=docker.io/shenxn/protonmail-bridge
ContainerName=protonmail-bridge
AutoUpdate=registry
Network=slirp4netns:allow_host_loopback=true
PublishPort=1025:25/tcp
PublishPort=1143:143/tcp
User=0:0
Volume=/home/tommi/docker-services/protonmail-bridge/config:/root:z

[Service]
Restart=always
TimeoutStartSec=900
```

2. Reboot 
3. podman exec -it protonmail-bridge cat /etc/hosts


### Describe the results you received

podman exec -it protonmail-bridge cat /etc/hosts
127.0.0.1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
10.0.2.100	31a0bbd8b981 protonmail-bridge


### Describe the results you expected

podman exec -it protonmail-bridge cat /etc/hosts
127.0.0.1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
192.168.0.11	host.containers.internal host.docker.internal
10.0.2.100	97e30cb3bc22 protonmail-bridge

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

OS: Bazzite, based on fedora silverblue. Not sure if this counts as 'Podman in a container'.

### Additional information

Changelogs from 4.7.0 to 4.7.2 don't seem to indicate any change or fix related to this.

comments.body:
Workaround:
Add this under [Container], it will fail the container start if the IP is not available.
`PodmanArgs=--add-host host.containers.internal:host-gateway`
Add this under [Service], it will sleep 1 second between restarts, which gives enough time for the IP to become available.
`RestartSec=1`

Did you make sure the network is fully set up by the time the container unit starts? If the ip address are not yet configured then there is obviously no way for podman to find a suitable ip address for host.containers.internal.

https://www.freedesktop.org/wiki/Software/systemd/NetworkTarget/ -> According to this, using 
```
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target
```
Should make sure that the network is up before the service starts. This is what I tried, but it failed. I don't know of another way.

Try running `ip addr` as part of ExecStartPre=
Also you can check if you even have any units configured in the online target with `sudo systemctl list-dependencies network-online.target`, there is no guarantee that these targets actually work it totally depends on the configuration of the networking tools.

ExecStartPre=ip addr 
```
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]: 1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]:     link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]:     inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]:        valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]:     inet6 ::1/128 scope host noprefixroute
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]:        valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]: 2: enp6s0: <NO-CARRIER,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq state DOWN group default qlen 1000
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]:     link/ether a8:5e:45:51:9a:26 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]: 3: enp7s0: <NO-CARRIER,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP> mtu 1500 qdisc mq state DOWN group default qlen 1000
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]:     link/ether a8:5e:45:51:9a:25 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]: 4: wlp5s0: <NO-CARRIER,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state DOWN group default qlen 1000
marras 06 16:11:00 bazzite [1857]:     link/ether 0e:15:49:f2:54:79 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff permaddr 50:eb:71:f9:75:a9

```
wlp5s0 is the one that should come up. 'state DOWN' implies it's not up?

sudo systemctl list-dependencies network-online.target
```
network-online.target
● └─NetworkManager-wait-online.service
```

But maybe this could be a Networkmanager bug and not a podman bug? I guess I should test a normal service to see if there is any difference.

check `systemctl status NetworkManager-wait-online.service` and `journalctl -r --unit NetworkManager.service`

This definitely does not look like a podman problem so I am going to close it, if the network is not setup at that point there is no way for us to guess the ip address.
